{{Quote|There are several likely gas giant worlds in remote systems that might have the Tibanna we need. I want you to survey those planets and find us a Tibanna deposit.|Captain Eedan, to a group of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Journal5" />}}
The '''Shasfath Cluster''' was an [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]] [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]]. Following the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Occupation of Bespin|occupation]] of [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] was forced to search for other sources of the important [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna gas]]. Alliance [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Eedan]] therefore tasked a group of Rebel agents with [[Scout/Legends|surveying]] [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]] planets in several [[Star system/Legends|star systems]], as well as in the Shasfath Cluster. The exploration of the [[Star/Legends|star]] cluster eventually proved fruitless, and the Rebel scouts continued with their search on the planet [[Taloraan/Legends|Taloraan]].

==Description==
The Shasfath Cluster was a remote [[Star cluster/Legends|star cluster]]<ref name="Journal5" /> located in the [[Inner Rim/Legends|Inner Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> The [[Star/Legends|star]] cluster contained at least one [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]–type [[Planet/Legends|planet]].<ref name="Journal5" />

==History==
{{Quote|Until recently, we could still buy Tibanna on the black market at Cloud City, on the planet Bespin. Now that the Empire has occupied that world, we need new supplies, and quickly.|Captain Eedan, to a group of Rebel Alliance agents|<ref name="Journal5" />}}
[[File:Cloud_City.jpg|thumb|right|200px|The Imperial occupation of Cloud City forced the Rebel Alliance to survey the Shasfath Cluster for Tibanna gas deposits.]]
Shortly after the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] [[Occupation of Bespin|occupied]] the [[Cloud City/Legends|Cloud City]] [[City/Legends|settlement]] on<ref name="Journal5" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> planet [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]]<ref name="Journal5" /> in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V ''The Empire Strikes Back'']], which includes the [[occupation of Bespin]], to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].</ref> the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], which until then had been acquiring [[Tibanna/Legends|Tibanna gas]] from Cloud City for use with [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] and [[Laser/Legends|laser]] weaponry, was forced to seek other sources of Tibanna. To that end, [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Eedan]] of the Alliance's [[Ordnance and Supply/Legends|Ordnance and Supply]] section tasked a group of Rebel agents with [[Scout/Legends|surveying]] [[Gas/Legends|gas]] giants in several Inner Rim and [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]] [[Star system/Legends|systems]] for any deposits of Tibanna.<ref name="Journal5" />

After finding no usable Tibanna deposits on planets within the [[Lequabis system/Legends|Lequabis]] and [[Poviduze system/Legends|Poviduze systems]]<ref name="Journal5" /> in the Expansion Region<ref name="EAOC" /> and the [[K'taktaxka system/Legends|K'taktaxka system]]<ref name="Journal5" /> in the Inner Rim,<ref name="EAOC" /> the Rebel scouts explored the Shasfath Cluster in their modified [[YT-700 transport|YT-700 Transport]], the ''[[Jackpot]]''. The group was unsuccessful there as well and continued its search on the Expansion Region planet [[Taloraan/Legends|Taloraan]].<ref name="Journal5" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Shasfath Cluster was first mentioned in "[[Wind Raiders of Taloraan]]," an article published in the [[February]] [[1995]] [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 5|fifth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]''. The article was written by [[James L. Cambias]] and contained a [[roleplaying game]] adventure for use with [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Journal5" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', written by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]], placed the star cluster within grid square J-14.<ref name="TEA" />
